title: Treasury Seeks Public Comment on GENIUS Act Notice of Proposed Rulemaking Concerning State-Level Regulatory Regimes | U.
summary: The U.S. Department of the Treasury issued a Notice of Proposed Rulemaking (NPRM) to establish principles for determining whether state-level stablecoin regulatory regimes are substantially similar to the federal framework under the GENIUS Act. Issuers with up to $10 billion in consolidated outstanding issuance may opt for state-level regulation if their state regime meets the substantially similar standard. This is Treasury's first proposed regulation to implement the GENIUS Act.
key_points: Treasury issued an NPRM seeking public comment on implementing the GENIUS Act's provisions for state-level regulatory regime equivalence
Stablecoin issuers with consolidated outstanding issuance not exceeding $10 billion may elect state-level regulation if substantially similar to federal framework
The NPRM establishes broad-based principles for determining substantial similarity through notice and comment rulemaking
This builds on an advance notice of proposed rulemaking from September 2025
Public comments will be accepted for 60 days following Federal Register publication
why_it_matters: This NPRM establishes the criteria by which state stablecoin regulatory regimes can qualify as equivalent to the federal framework, directly affecting which issuers can operate under state versus federal oversight. Stablecoin issuers and state regulators need to understand these principles to plan compliance pathways. The $10 billion threshold determines market coverage for the opt-in state regulatory approach.
market_structure_context: Stablecoin payment systems represent a growing component of digital payment infrastructure. This rulemaking establishes the regulatory architecture for how payment stablecoin issuers can be supervised at the state level, affecting market structure for digital payment systems.
important_numbers: $10,000,000,000 (10 billion dollar threshold for state-level regulatory opt-in)
